As temperatures drop in NYC, Code Blue is activated, while the NYPD increases patrols due to security concerns following an attack on Iran; meanwhile, Hoboken's PATH service resumes after a fire, and NYC restaurants were ordered closed for a week due to severe weather.

### Risks & Alerts

- Code Blue issued as temperatures drop in NYC and Newark; residents advised to take precautions. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMingFBVV95cUxQWnhLYThqQVEzN1FUTjViSjlrUE1OYmZIbnhaV1FlMjJIMjNNOWtkQ3dxemwxRXhVZHBVQVFMdmg1Z0hLZ2dvV2V4amlFLVRHWHM3U3YtbFNic2ZZRGtGUmFKVmRYYlprVGhQTVZ3R0ZnYlFVb2E5V2xRUXhXY3BrZUY1bG45bnRBbGxMeWtsWVNzckd6TDNqd0NqdU5LZw?oc=5)
- NYPD increases patrols in NYC due to heightened security concerns following recent Iran attack. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Hoboken PATH service has resumed after a track fire incident; normal operations are in effect. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- NYC restaurants ordered closed from February 20 to 27 due to health violations; check compliance before dining. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Man arrested for allegedly setting fires in his home to kill spiders, causing a bomb scare in NYC. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi_?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- Hochul's 'anti-fraud' scheme criticized for benefiting a large union at taxpayer expense. - Exclusive report on NYC's controversial approach to illegal hydrant parking. - ICE has detained over 120 children in NYC under controversial conditions. - Advocates seek $250M to save New York housing vouchers facing funding cuts. ### Community

- How Shelter, a legendary NYC dance party, celebrates 35 years of vibrant nightlife.
